high_consensus.description: Reclassifying sanitizer telemetry from error to warn has high operational ROI and very low implementation cost; it preserves signal while preventing alert-channel noise.

This closes a real boundary-condition gap where splitting can create a new start-of-string match context after sanitization; the risk is meaningful and the fix is straightforward and likely idempotent-safe.
A dev-mode invariant check for template idempotency is a strong regression guard against future character/template drift, with moderate complexity and good long-term safety payoff.

disputed.description: A dedicated sanitizer firing metric materially improves production observability and trend analysis at low implementation cost, especially compared with log scraping.
Adding a colonless secondary pattern may catch future SDK drift, but it reopens false-positive pressure and relies on speculative formats; useful idea but uncertain net value now.
Exporting pattern metadata for tests improves ordering/invariant coverage and reduces brittle string-based assertions, but this is a test ergonomics enhancement rather than runtime risk reduction.
Extracting a shared helper for error-template composition reduces drift across multiple call sites with low risk and moderate maintenance payoff.

blockers.concern: Detection regexes are brittle to upstream format drift because most specific patterns require exact prefixes and numeric status codes (for example `API Error: \d+`, `bedrock chat error: \d+`). Variants like `API error: overloaded`, `upstream API Error: ...`, or statusless messages can bypass substitution.
blockers.why_matters: A bypass reintroduces raw provider/internal error leakage at the chat boundary, breaking the stated in-character-only invariant.
blockers.recommendation: Broaden matching to tolerate case/prefix variants and non-numeric tails, and add a fallback heuristic for common leak tokens (`api error`, `internal server error`, provider names) with tight false-positive guards.

blockers.concern: Homoglyph/confusable bypass remains open by design (deferred), and NFKC + `\p{Cf}` stripping does not catch script-mixed lookalikes (for example Cyrillic/Greek letters in `API Error`).
blockers.why_matters: An attacker or malformed upstream string can evade pattern matching while still presenting a visually identical leaked error to users.
blockers.recommendation: Implement script-confusable folding or mixed-script detection on the probe path, then test adversarial variants (Cyrillic A/P/I, Greek Ε, etc.) against all blocker patterns.
